steps: Add TOKEN_STDIN_READ, TOKEN_FILE_READ, TOKEN_FILE_WRITE, TOKEN_FILE_APPEND to token types
Tokenize <| as TOKEN_STDIN_READ
Tokenize <. as TOKEN_FILE_READ
Tokenize .> as TOKEN_FILE_WRITE
Tokenize .>> as TOKEN_FILE_APPEND
Disambiguate .>> vs .>
Disambiguate <. vs <- vs <= vs <<

description: Parser produces correct AST for stdin read, file read, file write, file append
steps: Parse line, err = <| into MultiAssignStmt with StdinReadExpr
Parse data, err = <. "path" into MultiAssignStmt with FileReadExpr
Parse data, err = <. expr into MultiAssignStmt with FileReadExpr
Parse .> "path" data into FileWriteStmt
Parse .> expr expr into FileWriteStmt
Parse .>> "path" data into FileAppendStmt

description: Runtime I/O functions: StdinRead, FileRead, FileWrite, FileAppend, Split, ToNum in io.go
steps: StdinRead reads one line via bufio.Scanner, returns (line, [0]) on success
StdinRead returns ("", [1]) on EOF or error
FileRead reads entire file, returns (content, [0]) on success
FileRead returns ("", [1]) on missing file
FileWrite writes data to file with 0644 perms, panics on error
FileAppend appends data to file, panics on error
Split splits string by separator, returns SlopValue array of strings
Split with empty separator returns original string as-is
Split panics if args are not single-element strings
ToNum parses int64 then float64, returns (val, [0]) on success
ToNum returns ([], [1]) on parse failure

description: Codegen lowers I/O AST nodes to sloprt calls, adds split/to_num to builtin dispatch
steps: Lower StdinReadExpr to sloprt.StdinRead()
Lower FileReadExpr to sloprt.FileRead(path)
Lower FileWriteStmt to sloprt.FileWrite(path, data)
Lower FileAppendStmt to sloprt.FileAppend(path, data)
Add split to builtin map → sloprt.Split
Add to_num to dual-return builtin handling → sloprt.ToNum
MultiAssignStmt recognizes to_num as dual-return builtin

description: E2E tests for file I/O, stdin, split, to_num covering happy paths, errors, and edge cases
steps: File write then read: .> creates file, <. reads it back
File append: .>> appends to existing file
File read missing: <. returns err [1]
File write overwrites existing content
Split by space: split("a b c", " ") returns [a, b, c]
Split by newline: split("a\nb", "\n") returns [a, b]
Split empty separator: split("hello", "") returns [hello]
to_num int: to_num("42") returns ([42], [0])
to_num float: to_num("3.14") returns ([3.14], [0])
to_num failure: to_num("abc") returns ([], [1])
Combined: write file, read, split, to_num pipeline
File I/O with variables as path and data

description: Example file fileio.slop demonstrating all Phase 6 features
steps: fileio.slop: file write, read, append, split, to_num, error handling
